fields.summary: Support all types of placement constraint support for Capacity Scheduler
Improvements to Rich Placement Constraints in YARN

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: AppPlacementAllocator currently only supports intra-app anti-affinity placement constraints, once YARN-8002 and YARN-8013 are resolved, it needs to support inter-app constraints too. Also, this may require some refactoring on the existing code logic. Use this JIRA to track.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Add more E2E tests for placement constraints

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

fields.summary: Support specifying placement constraint for task containers in SLS

status.description: A patch for this issue has been uploaded to JIRA by a contributor.
status.name: Patch Available

comments.body:
Uploaded v1 patch, changes outline:

Added some UT in {{TestSchedulingRequestContainerAllocation}} to cover:
 * Inter-app constraints by namespace, including all/not-self/app-id/app-tag
 * Inter-app composite constraints, specify constraints like AND(P1, P2)
 * Inter-app constraints with multiple source targets

Simplified validations in {{SingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator}}, removed unnecessary checks including
 * Only allow single constraint
 * Only support node scope
 * Only support anti-affinity

Remove unnecessary validations in {{TestSingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator}} accordingly

| (/) *{color:green}+1 overall{color}* |

Hi [~leftnoteasy] 

Could you please help to review?

FYI, I think we need a Jira to revisit how to support node-partition in PC, I can see some problems/limitation in current approach (which only supports affinity to a partition).

This message was automatically generated.
Hi [~sunilg], please help to review, thanks.

Thanks for the patch [~cheersyang]

Some quick questions:
 # To support affinity for node partition, i think current patch supports to fetch the partition from incoming expression. Setting in targetNodePartition is enough?
 # I just saw that {{if (!(ac instanceof PlacementConstraint.SingleConstraint))}} is removed. But this check is needed, correct?

Hi [~sunilg]

To answer your questions:
# Current patch is preserving the behavior of current code base how node-partition is supported, but I don't think that is enough. The node-partition in PC needs some more work, I was trying to keep patch independent so haven't touched that part. I prefer to open another JIRA to get that done.
# No, that check is not needed. It assumes only {{SingleConstraint}} is supported but actually we have everything else done to support {{CompositeConstraint}}, such as AND or OR. We don't need it anymore.

Thanks

Thanks [~cheersyang]. Looks fine to me.

Committing shortly. I think we can commit this only to trunk alone, correct?

Hi [~sunilg], thanks
{quote}I think we can commit this only to trunk alone
{quote}
I am OK with that. We can have the full support of PC and node-attributes in 3.2 release line.
Thanks [~cheersyang]

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #14818 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/14818/])
YARN-8015. Support all types of placement constraint support for (sunilg: rev 1ac01444a24faee6f74f2e83d9521eb4e0be651b)
* (edit) hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager/src/test/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/MockRM.java
* (edit) hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/scheduler/placement/SingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator.java
* (edit) hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager/src/test/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/scheduler/capacity/TestSchedulingRequestContainerAllocation.java
* (edit) hadoop-yarn-project/hadoop-yarn/hadoop-yarn-server/hadoop-yarn-server-resourcemanager/src/test/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/server/resourcemanager/scheduler/placement/TestSingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator.java
Thanks [~sunilg]!

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body:
Uploaded v1 patch, changes outline:

Added some UT in {{TestSchedulingRequestContainerAllocation}} to cover:
 * Inter-app constraints by namespace, including all/not-self/app-id/app-tag
 * Inter-app composite constraints, specify constraints like AND(P1, P2)
 * Inter-app constraints with multiple source targets

Simplified validations in {{SingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator}}, removed unnecessary checks including
 * Only allow single constraint
 * Only support node scope
 * Only support anti-affinity

Remove unnecessary validations in {{TestSingleConstraintAppPlacementAllocator}} accordingly

Hi [~sunilg], please help to review, thanks.

Thanks for the patch [~cheersyang]

Some quick questions:
 # To support affinity for node partition, i think current patch supports to fetch the partition from incoming expression. Setting in targetNodePartition is enough?
 # I just saw that {{if (!(ac instanceof PlacementConstraint.SingleConstraint))}} is removed. But this check is needed, correct?

Hi [~sunilg]

To answer your questions:
# Current patch is preserving the behavior of current code base how node-partition is supported, but I don't think that is enough. The node-partition in PC needs some more work, I was trying to keep patch independent so haven't touched that part. I prefer to open another JIRA to get that done.
# No, that check is not needed. It assumes only {{SingleConstraint}} is supported but actually we have everything else done to support {{CompositeConstraint}}, such as AND or OR. We don't need it anymore.

Thanks

Thanks [~cheersyang]. Looks fine to me.

Committing shortly. I think we can commit this only to trunk alone, correct?

Hi [~sunilg], thanks
{quote}I think we can commit this only to trunk alone
{quote}
I am OK with that. We can have the full support of PC and node-attributes in 3.2 release line.
Thanks [~cheersyang]
